Mid-level (2+ years preferred)
English-friendly

About the Company:
Jamie is an early-stage startup building an AI meeting note platform. Core team membership with significant influence. Fast-growth learning environment with modern development tools and AI agents.

About the Role:
Turn user needs and product ideas into robust, well-crafted code and prompts for an AI meeting note platform. Iterate rapidly using user feedback and analytics to drive product metrics. Collaborate with founders to define, experiment, and enhance user experience.

Key Responsibilities:
- Turn user needs and product ideas into robust, well-crafted code and prompts
- Iterate rapidly using user feedback and analytics to drive product metrics
- Collaborate with founders to define, experiment, and enhance user experience
- Ensure delivery of reliable, high-quality products

Required Skills:
- Strong product mindset with deep user experience focus
- ML/AI pipeline optimization, MLOps, and prompt engineering experience
- Proven software development background (2+ years preferred)
- Adaptability and quick learning in fast-paced, evolving environments

Nice-to-Have:
- Side projects or demonstrable past work examples

Benefits:
- Core team membership with significant influence
- Fast-growth learning environment
- Modern development tools and AI agents
- Competitive salary and VSOP (stock options)
- Beautiful Berlin Mitte office
- Team events and offsites

Application deadline: May 26, 2026

instruction: Extract structured information from the following job description. Return valid JSON with these fields: title, company, location, work_model, seniority, required_skills (list), nice_to_have (list), salary, language.
input:
AI Engineer (LLMs + Knowledge Graphs) (m/f/d)
Pinnipedia Technologies GmbH
Berlin / Ketzin (hybrid) | Full-time | €60,000-85,000

About the Company
Pinnipedia Technologies GmbH is a Berlin startup building a cloud platform that automates and assists the creation of audit-ready IT-security concepts for German/EU standards (e.g., BSI-Grundschutz, C5). The company is IGP-funded (2025/26) and co-develops with FU Berlin and pilot users from industry and security consulting.

About the Role
AI Engineer to turn messy inputs into structured knowledge and reliable answers. You will design and operate knowledge-graph + LLM (RAG) pipelines, model/ingest domain ontologies, and own evaluation so we can ship trustworthy features.

Key Responsibilities
- Model the domain (ontology/taxonomy), build ETL into a graph store, and author queries (SPARQL/Cypher) to surface graph facts and relationships in features
- Design retrieval and answer generation workflows (indexing, chunking, reranking), and orchestrate prompts/tools while balancing KG, vector search, and business rules
- Define and track retrieval/answer metrics (e.g., precision/recall, faithfulness)
- Own evaluation so we can ship trustworthy features

Required Skills
- Strong Python and data engineering fundamentals
- Hands-on with knowledge graphs (ontology design + queries) and a graph DB
- Practical RAG experience (indexing, retrieval, evaluation)
- Testing mindset (pytest), version control, and clear documentation
- English required (German nice-to-have)

Nice-to-Have
- Observability for ML/LLM systems; simple dashboards for quality metrics
- Cloud basics (AWS/Azure), containers (Docker), CI/CD

Application Note Required
Submit CV (PDF) and a short note (max 200 words) describing how you would design a KG-backed RAG pipeline (ontology scope, indexing, retrieval, and evaluation).

Interview Process
20-min intro → 90-min practical (graph modeling + retrieval evaluation) → 45-min team chat → references
output:
{
  "title": "AI Engineer (LLMs + Knowledge Graphs)",
  "company": "Pinnipedia Technologies GmbH",
  "location": "Berlin / Ketzin, Germany",
  "work_model": "hybrid",
  "seniority": "mid-level",
  "required_skills": [
    "Python",
    "knowledge graphs",
    "ontology design",
    "SPARQL/Cypher",
    "RAG",
    "evaluation metrics"
  ],
  "nice_to_have": [
    "ML/LLM observability",
    "AWS/Azure",
    "Docker",
    "CI/CD"
  ],
  "salary": "60,000-85,000 EUR",
  "language": "English"
}

instruction: Extract structured information from the following job description. Return valid JSON with these fields: title, company, location, work_model, seniority, required_skills (list), nice_to_have (list), salary, language.
input:
Applied AI, Forward Deployed Machine Learning Engineer - EMEA
Mistral AI
Paris / London / Luxembourg / Marseille / Amsterdam / Munich / Lausanne
Solutions / Full-Time / On-Site

About The Job
Mistral AI is seeking a Applied AI Engineer to facilitate the adoption of its products among customers and collaborate with them to address complex technical challenges.
The Applied AI team is Mistral's customer-facing technical organization. We work directly with enterprise clients from pre-sales through implementation to deploy cutting-edge AI solutions that deliver measurable business impact.
Our team combines deep ML expertise with strong customer engagement skills, operating like startup CTOs who own end-to-end project execution. By joining the team you'll will bridge the gap between cutting-edge AI research and real-world enterprise applications, ensuring our solutions are robust, scalable, and aligned with both customer needs and Mistral's technological vision.

What you will do
- You'll individually help deploy into production use cases with a considerable business impact across various industries.
- You'll work on state-of-the-art GenAI applications from consumer products to industrial use cases, driving with our customers a crucial technological transformation.
- You'll work in collaboration with our researchers, other AI engineers, product engineers on our most complex customer projects involving complex fine-tuning, state-of-the-art LLM applications, and contributing to our open-source codebases for tasks such as inference and fine-tuning.
- You'll be involved in pre-sales calls to understand potential clients' needs, challenges, and aspirations. You will provide technical guidance on our products and explain Mistral technologies to various stakeholders.
- Your collaboration with our product and science team to improve continuously our product and model capabilities based on customers' feedback

How We Work in Applied AI
- We care about people and outputs.
- What matters is what you ship, not the time you spend on it
- Bureaucracy is where urgency goes to vanish. You talk to whoever you need to talk to. The best idea wins, whether it comes from a principal engineer or someone in their first week.
- Always ask why. The best solutions come from deep understanding, not from copying what worked before
- We say what we mean. Feedback is direct, timely, and given because we care.
- No politics. Low ego, high standards.
- We embrace an unstructured environment and find joy in it.

About you
- You are fluent in English
- You have 2+ years as a technical individual contributor (data scientist or software engineer) on AI-based products
- You have proven experience in AI or machine learning product implementation with APIs, back-end and front-end interfaces.
- You have experience in Fine Tuning LLMs, tackling advanced RAG or agentic use cases
- You have deep understanding of concepts and algorithms underlying machine learning and LLMs
- You have strong technical coding skills in Python
- You hold strong communication skills with an ability to explain complex technical concepts in simple terms with technical and non-technical audiences

Ideally you have:
- Contributed to open-source projects in particular in the space of LLMs
- Experience as a Customer Engineer, Forward Deployed Engineer, Sales Engineer, Solutions Architect or Technical Product Manager
- You have experience with deep learning with Pytorch

instruction: Extract structured information from the following job description. Return valid JSON with these fields: title, company, location, work_model, seniority, required_skills (list), nice_to_have (list), salary, language.
input:
Applied AI Engineer, Fullstack Software Engineer - EMEA
Mistral AI
Paris / Marseille / London / Luxembourg / Lausanne / Amsterdam
Solutions / Full-Time / Hybrid

About Mistral
At Mistral AI, we believe in the power of AI to simplify tasks, save time, and enhance learning and creativity. Our technology is designed to integrate seamlessly into daily working life.
We democratize AI through high-performance, optimized, open-source and cutting-edge models, products and solutions. Our comprehensive AI platform is designed to meet enterprise needs, whether on-premises or in cloud environments. Our offerings include le Chat, the AI assistant for life and work.
We are a dynamic, collaborative team passionate about AI and its potential to transform society.
Our diverse workforce thrives in competitive environments and is committed to driving innovation. Our teams are distributed between France, USA, UK, Germany and Singapore. We are creative, low-ego and team-spirited.

About The Job
Mistral AI is seeking an Applied AI Engineer to facilitate the adoption of its products among customers and collaborate with them to address complex technical challenges.
The Applied AI Engineer - Software Engineer will be an integral part of our Applied AI Engineering team, which is dedicated to driving the successful deployment of Mistral AI products. They will work hand-in-hand with customers from the pre-sale stage to post-implementation, ensuring our solutions meet and exceed client expectations.

What you will do
- Collaborate closely with researchers, AI engineers, and product engineers on complex customer projects, integrating cutting-edge AI models into clients' software products.
- Design, develop, and maintain scalable and robust full-stack applications, ensuring seamless integration between front-end and back-end systems.
- Develop complex use cases with our customers, providing guidance and ensuring the best production integration with back-end and front-end interfaces.
- Collaborate with our product and science team to continuously improve our product and model capabilities based on customers' feedback.

About you
- You are fluent in English
- You hold a degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field.
- You have 2+ years as a technical individual contributor on cutting-edge technologies
- You have strong technical coding skills in Python and TypeScript
- You have experience with front-end frameworks such as React, NextJS, or VueJS.
- You have experience with back-end technologies such as NodeJS.
- You have a solid understanding of software development principles, including design patterns, data structures, and algorithms.
- You hold strong communication skills with an ability to explain complex technical concepts in simple terms with technical and non-technical audiences

Ideally you have:
- Experience with LLM/GenAI models
- Contributed to open-source projects or libraries
- Experience as a Customer Engineer, Forward Deployed Engineer, Sales Engineer, Solutions Architect or Technical Product Manager

Benefits
- Competitive cash salary and equity
- Daily lunch vouchers
- Sport: Monthly contribution to Gympass
- Transportation: Monthly contribution to mobility pass
- Health: Full health insurance for you and your family
- Parental: Generous parental leave policy
- Visa sponsorship
output:
{
  "title": "Applied AI Engineer, Fullstack Software Engineer",
  "company": "Mistral AI",
  "location": "Paris / Marseille / London / Amsterdam",
  "work_model": "hybrid",
  "seniority": "mid-level",
  "required_skills": [
    "Python",
    "TypeScript",
    "React",
    "Next.js",
    "Node.js",
    "full-stack development"
  ],
  "nice_to_have": [
    "LLM/GenAI",
    "open-source contributions",
    "customer engineering"
  ],
  "salary": "",
  "language": "English"
}

instruction: Extract structured information from the following job description. Return valid JSON with these fields: title, company, location, work_model, seniority, required_skills (list), nice_to_have (list), salary, language.
input:
AI Software Engineer (m/f/d)
Sunhat GmbH
Cologne HQ / Berlin / Hamburg / Remote (CET/CEST timezone)
Permanent, Full-time
Seniority: Experienced (2-5 years)

About the Company:
Sunhat is an AI-driven SaaS company rethinking how businesses approach sustainability performance and reporting. Building a next-generation platform to deliver scalability and verifiable data across all ESG requirements. Raised EUR 14.2M total (EUR 9.2M Series A). 35 employees, growing to 70.

About the Role:
Drive generative AI initiatives from concept through deployment. Evaluate and integrate large language models into production systems, orchestrate AI agents, build text generation and semantic similarity features, and optimize for scalability and security.

Key Responsibilities:
- Evaluate and integrate LLMs into production systems
- Orchestrate AI agents
- Build text generation and semantic similarity features
- Optimize for scalability and security
- Collaborate with software engineers and sustainability experts on platform integration

Required Skills:
- Production experience with GenAI/LLMs (Gemini, Mistral, Claude, Llama)
- TypeScript expertise with production system integration
- Agentic workflows and RAG systems
- CI/CD practices with GitHub
- Modern LLMOps practices and Test-Driven Development
- B2B SaaS implementation experience
- Fluent English communication

Nice-to-Have:
- Python knowledge
- Angular and Nest.js experience
- Vertex AI experience
- Data privacy and performance optimization in cloud ML environments

Benefits:
- Flexible remote work (CET/CEST timezone)
- Competitive salary with equity options (EUR 65,000-95,000)
- Family-friendly flexibility
- Professional development budget
- EUR 50 monthly wellness benefit

Language: English (fluency required)
output:
{
  "title": "AI Software Engineer",
  "company": "Sunhat GmbH",
  "location": "Cologne / Remote, Germany",

    "Python",
    "LLMs",
    "RAG",
    "agentic systems",
    "FastAPI",
    "Docker"

    "Angular",
    "Nest.js",
    "Vertex AI"
  ],

  "language": "English"
}
